name: Eel (Python-JS Desktop App Bridge)

performance.notes: Local WebSocket communication; fast for UI interactions

agent_readiness.notes: Build desktop apps with Python backend and HTML/CSS/JS frontend. import eel. @eel.expose def say_hello(name): return f'Hello {name}'. eel.init('web'). eel.start('index.html'). In JS: eel.say_hello('World')(cb). Call JS from Python: eel.my_js_function(arg)(). Expose JS: eel.expose(cb_fn). Bundling: PyInstaller + eel. Uses Chrome/Chromium or Edge in app mode. Alternative to Electron for Python. Simpler than Tauri for Python devs. Limited to single-window apps well. Not maintained as actively since 2022.

gotchas: Requires Chrome/Chromium/Edge installed — not fully self-contained
Single-threaded Python GIL applies; heavy computation blocks UI
Development has slowed since 2022 — consider alternatives
Not suitable for production-grade desktop apps with complex UI
Bundling with PyInstaller adds complexity and size
best_when: Quick Python desktop apps with rich HTML/CSS/JS frontend
Developer tools and internal utilities with web-based UI
Python developers who prefer web tech over tkinter/PyQt
Simple data visualization apps where Streamlit is overkill
avoid_when: Production desktop apps (use Tauri, Electron, or PyQt6)
Apps needing offline-first without browser dependency
When NiceGUI or Gradio covers the use case
summary: Eel builds Python desktop apps with HTML/CSS/JS frontend. import eel; @eel.expose def fn(): ...; eel.init('web'); eel.start('index.html'). Python ↔ JS via WebSocket. Uses Chrome/Chromium in app mode. MIT. Not actively maintained since 2022. Good for quick internal tools. For production use Tauri or PyQt6. NiceGUI/Gradio better for data apps.
